title: TMNRED, A Chinese Language EEG Dataset for Fuzzy Semantic Target Identification in Natural Reading Environments
description: TMNRED is a Chinese language EEG dataset comprising recordings from 30 healthy native speakers during natural reading tasks designed to investigate semantic processing mechanisms. Participants read text stimuli (15-20 characters) presented as news headlines or short sentences, with each participant completing 8 blocks of 400 trials. The dataset includes both raw and preprocessed EEG data organized according to BIDS standards. Sensor-level EEG analyses have been performed to examine neural responses to target and non-target semantic items.
methods_description: EEG recordings were obtained from 30 right-handed participants (mean age 22.07 years, SD 2.7; 18 females, 12 males) during self-paced reading of text stimuli. Each trial lasted approximately 2.2 seconds including fixation cross and inter-stimulus intervals. Participants completed 8 blocks of 400 trials total. Sensor-level EEG analyses were performed to examine responses to target and non-target words.

funding_references.funder_name: National Key R&D Program of China
National Natural Science Foundation of China
